name: coatnet

members.name: CoAtNetConfig
members.signature: CoAtNetConfig(num_classes: int = 1000, in_channels: int = 3, image_size: int = 224, variant: str = 'coatnet_0', blocks_per_stage: tuple[int, ...] = (2, 3, 5, 2), dims: tuple[int, ...] = (96, 192, 384, 768), stem_width: int = 64, attn_heads: tuple[int, ...] = (12, 24), mbconv_expand: int = 4, head_hidden_size: int | None = 768, dropout: float = 0.0, mixed_s3: tuple[int, int, int] | None = None)

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(num_classes: int = 1000, in_channels: int = 3, image_size: int = 224, variant: str = 'coatnet_0', blocks_per_stage: tuple[int, ...] = (2, 3, 5, 2), dims: tuple[int, ...] = (96, 192, 384, 768), stem_width: int = 64, attn_heads: tuple[int, ...] = (12, 24), mbconv_expand: int = 4, head_hidden_size: int | None = 768, dropout: float = 0.0, mixed_s3: tuple[int, int, int] | None = None)

members.summary: Configuration dataclass for every CoAtNet variant.
members.extended:
`CoAtNetConfig` is an immutable container that fully specifies
the architecture of a CoAtNet (Dai et al., 2021).  CoAtNet uses
*four stages* after a two-conv stem, with the first two stages
being depthwise-convolutional (MBConv) and the last two stages
being relative-attention transformer blocks.

parameters.name: num_classes
parameters.description:
Number of output classes for the classification head.  Defaults
to `1000` (ImageNet-1k).

parameters.name: in_channels
parameters.description: Number of input image channels.  Defaults to `3` (RGB).

parameters.name: image_size
parameters.description:
Spatial side length of the square input image (used to build
the fixed-size relative-position bias tables in T-stages).
Defaults to `224`.

parameters.name: variant
parameters.description:
Informational label identifying the canonical CoAtNet variant
(`"coatnet_0"` through `"coatnet_7"`).  Defaults to
`"coatnet_0"`.

parameters.name: blocks_per_stage
parameters.description:
Number of blocks in each of the four stages
$(S_1, S_2, S_3, S_4)$.  Defaults to `(2, 3, 5, 2)`
(CoAtNet-0).

parameters.name: dims
parameters.description:
Channel widths for each of the four stages.  Defaults to
`(96, 192, 384, 768)` (CoAtNet-0).

parameters.name: stem_width
parameters.description:
Output channels of the two-layer convolutional stem (before
$S_1$).  Defaults to `64`.

parameters.name: attn_heads
parameters.description:
Number of attention heads for each of the two T-stages
$(S_3, S_4)$.  Defaults to `(12, 24)`, derived from
`dim / 32` with head dim 32.

parameters.name: mbconv_expand
parameters.description:
Expansion ratio of the inverted bottleneck inside MBConv blocks
(`mid = out_ch * expand`).  Defaults to `4`.

parameters.name: head_hidden_size
parameters.description:
If not `None`, inserts an extra pre-classifier linear layer
of the given width with a tanh activation between the pooled
feature and the final classifier.  Defaults to `768`
(matches the reference recipe).

parameters.name: dropout
parameters.description:
Dropout probability inside the classifier head.  Defaults to
`0.0`.

examples:
Build a CoAtNet-0 configuration with a 10-class head for CIFAR-10:
>>> from lucid.models.vision.coatnet import CoAtNetConfig
>>> cfg = CoAtNetConfig(num_classes=10)
>>> cfg.dims, cfg.blocks_per_stage, cfg.num_classes
((96, 192, 384, 768), (2, 3, 5, 2), 10)
notes:
The canonical CoAtNet-0 (registered as `coatnet_0`) has
approximately **25.6M parameters** and reaches **81.6% top-1 on
ImageNet-1k** at 224x224 (Dai et al., 2021, Table 5).

Reference: Zihang Dai *et al.*, *"CoAtNet: Marrying Convolution
and Attention for All Data Sizes"*, NeurIPS 2021,
[arXiv:2106.04803](https://arxiv.org/abs/2106.04803).

attributes.name: model_type
attributes.description: Constant string `"coatnet"` used by the model registry.

members.name: CoAtNet
members.extended:
CoAtNet is a *hybrid* backbone that interleaves depthwise
convolutions and relative self-attention in a single four-stage
pyramid (preceded by a two-layer convolutional stem).  The first
two stages ($S_1, S_2$) use *MBConv* blocks (squeeze-and-
excitation, expansion ratio 4) and the last two stages
($S_3, S_4$) use *relative-attention* transformer blocks
operating on flattened token sequences:

$$
\mathrm{Attn}(Q, K, V)_{ij} = \mathrm{softmax}\!\left(
    \frac{Q_i K_j^\top}{\sqrt{d}} + r_{i - j}
\right) V_j,
$$

where $r_{i-j}$ is a learned bias indexed by the *relative*
spatial offset between tokens.  This recovers the translation
equivariance that convolutions provide while still permitting
global, data-dependent mixing.  Each stage downsamples
$2\times$, so the final feature map is
$(B, d_{S_4}, H/32, W/32)$.

`forward_features` returns the raw spatial feature map from
the last attention stage.  Use this backbone when you need
multi-scale or spatial features for detection / segmentation; for
end-to-end classification use
`CoAtNetForImageClassification`.
examples:
Build a CoAtNet-0 backbone and run a forward pass:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.coatnet import CoAtNet, CoAtNetConfig
>>> model = CoAtNet(CoAtNetConfig())
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> feat = model.forward_features(x)
>>> feat.shape                       # (B, dims[-1], H/32, W/32)
(1, 768, 7, 7)
notes:
Reference: Zihang Dai *et al.*, *"CoAtNet: Marrying Convolution
and Attention for All Data Sizes"*, NeurIPS 2021,
[arXiv:2106.04803](https://arxiv.org/abs/2106.04803).

members.name: CoAtNetForImageClassification

methods.name: forward
methods.signature: forward(x: Tensor, labels: Tensor | None = None)

members.summary: CoAtNet with a linear classification head (Dai et al., 2021).
members.extended:
Wraps the same conv + attention trunk as `CoAtNet` (stem
+ two MBConv stages + two relative-attention transformer stages)
and adds the standard reference recipe head: global average pool
→ LayerNorm → optional pre-logits Linear + Tanh → linear
classifier.

$$
\text{logits} = W_{\text{cls}}\,
    \mathrm{Tanh}\!\bigl(W_{\text{pre}}\,
    \mathrm{LN}(\mathrm{GAP}(z^{S_4}))\bigr) + b_{\text{cls}}.
$$

Pass `labels` to `forward` to compute the cross-entropy
loss in the same pass.

parameters.name: config
parameters.description:
Architecture specification.  Must set `num_classes` to the
desired number of output categories.  Set
`head_hidden_size=None` to drop the pre-logits projection.
See `CoAtNetConfig`.

examples:
End-to-end inference with the default CoAtNet-0 classifier:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.coatnet import (
...     CoAtNetConfig, CoAtNetForImageClassification,
... )
>>> model = CoAtNetForImageClassification(CoAtNetConfig())
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape
(1, 1000)
notes:
Reference: Zihang Dai *et al.*, *"CoAtNet: Marrying Convolution
and Attention for All Data Sizes"*, NeurIPS 2021.  CoAtNet-0
reaches **81.6% top-1 on ImageNet-1k** at 224x224 (Table 5).

attributes.name: avgpool
attributes.description: $1 \times 1$ adaptive average pool over spatial dims.

attributes.name: norm
attributes.description: LayerNorm applied to the pooled feature.

attributes.name: pre_logits
attributes.description:
Either `Linear + Tanh` (when `config.head_hidden_size` is
set) or an identity `nn.Sequential`.

attributes.name: classifier
attributes.description:
Final linear projection of width `(num_classes, head_in)`
where `head_in` is either `config.head_hidden_size` or
`config.dims[-1]`.

members.name: coatnet_0
members.extended:
Builds the canonical *CoAtNet-0* configuration:
`blocks_per_stage=(2, 3, 5, 2)`, `dims=(96, 192, 384, 768)`,
`stem_width=64`, `attn_heads=(12, 24)`,
`head_hidden_size=768`.  The first two stages are MBConv with
expansion ratio 4 and the last two stages are relative-attention
transformers with head dim 32.  Approximately **25.6M parameters**.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
If `True`, loads ImageNet-1k pretrained weights when
available in the model zoo.  Defaults to `False`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Keyword overrides applied on top of the canonical CoAtNet-0
config.  Common examples: `image_size=384`, `in_channels=1`,
`head_hidden_size=None`.  Each override must match a field of
`CoAtNetConfig`.

returns.description:
A `CoAtNet` backbone returning a
$(B, 768, H/32, W/32)$ spatial feature map.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.coatnet import coatnet_0
>>> model = coatnet_0()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> feat = model.forward_features(x)
>>> feat.shape
(1, 768, 7, 7)
notes:
CoAtNet-0 reaches **81.6% top-1 on ImageNet-1k** at 224x224 (Dai
et al., 2021, Table 5).  See
[arXiv:2106.04803](https://arxiv.org/abs/2106.04803).

members.name: coatnet_0_cls
members.extended:
Combines the `coatnet_0` backbone with the standard CoAtNet
head: global average pool → LayerNorm → `Linear(768) + Tanh` →
final linear classifier.  Default output is `num_classes=1000`
(ImageNet-1k).  Approximately **25.6M parameters**.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
If `True`, loads ImageNet-1k pretrained weights when
available.  Defaults to `False`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Keyword overrides on top of the canonical CoAtNet-0 config.
Commonly used: `num_classes` to switch label space,
`image_size` to fine-tune at a different resolution.

returns.description:
Classifier returning `ImageClassificationOutput` whose
`logits` has shape `(B, num_classes)`.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.coatnet import coatnet_0_cls
>>> model = coatnet_0_cls(num_classes=1000)
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape
(1, 1000)
notes:
CoAtNet-0 reaches **81.6% top-1 on ImageNet-1k** at 224x224 (Dai
et al., 2021, Table 5).  Larger variants (CoAtNet-3 / -4) reach
**84.5% / 85.8%** at 384x384 after JFT-3B pretraining
(Table 1, headline result).

members.name: coatnet_1
members.extended:
Same channel widths as CoAtNet-0 but with substantially deeper
stages: `blocks_per_stage=(2, 6, 14, 2)`, `dims=(96, 192, 384, 768)`,
`stem_width=64`, `attn_heads=(12, 24)`.  Approximately **42M
parameters**.
notes:
Reaches **83.3% ImageNet-1k top-1** at 224×224 (Table 5, NeurIPS
2021).  See [arXiv:2106.04803](https://arxiv.org/abs/2106.04803).

members.name: coatnet_1_cls
members.extended:
Backbone + standard CoAtNet head (GAP → LayerNorm → Linear+Tanh →
classifier).  Approximately **42M parameters**, 83.3% ImageNet-1k
top-1 at 224x224 (Table 5).

members.name: coatnet_2
members.extended:
Wider variant: `blocks_per_stage=(2, 6, 14, 2)`,
`dims=(128, 256, 512, 1024)`, `stem_width=128`,
`attn_heads=(16, 32)`.  Approximately **75M parameters**, **84.1%
ImageNet-1k top-1** at 224×224 (Table 5).

members.name: coatnet_2_cls
members.extended:
Wider variant — `dims=(128, 256, 512, 1024)` — with the standard
CoAtNet head.  Approximately **75M parameters**, 84.1% ImageNet-1k
top-1 at 224x224 (Table 5).

members.name: coatnet_3
members.extended:
Larger variant: `blocks_per_stage=(2, 6, 14, 2)`,
`dims=(192, 384, 768, 1536)`, `stem_width=192`,
`attn_heads=(24, 48)`.  Approximately **168M parameters**, **84.5%
ImageNet-1k top-1** at 224×224 (Table 5).  With ImageNet-21k or
JFT-3B pretraining the paper reports 86.5% / 87.8% at 384x384.

members.name: coatnet_3_cls
members.extended:
Larger variant — `dims=(192, 384, 768, 1536)` — with the standard
CoAtNet head.  Approximately **168M parameters**, 84.5% ImageNet-1k
top-1 at 224x224 (Table 5).

members.name: coatnet_4
members.extended:
Even deeper variant: `blocks_per_stage=(2, 12, 28, 2)`,
`dims=(192, 384, 768, 1536)`, `stem_width=192`,
`attn_heads=(24, 48)`.  Approximately **275M parameters**, **85.0%
ImageNet-1k top-1** at 224×224 (Table 5).  Paper headline with
JFT-3B pretrain reaches 88.4% at 512x512.

members.name: coatnet_4_cls
members.extended:
Deepest of the CoAtNet-0..4 series — same dims as CoAtNet-3 but with
`blocks_per_stage=(2, 12, 28, 2)`.  Approximately **275M
parameters**, 85.0% ImageNet-1k top-1 at 224x224 (Table 5).

members.name: coatnet_5
members.extended:
Widest paper-cited variant: `blocks_per_stage=(2, 12, 28, 2)`,
`dims=(256, 512, 1280, 2048)`, `stem_width=192`,
`attn_heads=(40, 64)`.  Approximately **688M parameters**, **85.8%
ImageNet-1k top-1** at 224×224 (Table 5).  With JFT-3B pretraining
the paper reports 89.0% at 512x512.
notes:
Memory footprint at default `image_size=224` is non-trivial — a
forward pass of a single 3×224×224 input materialises stage-3/4
activations on the order of `B × D × H/32 × W/32` channels with
D=2048.  Instantiating + running this variant on a 16 GB host is
near-the-edge; prefer measuring on a larger GPU.

members.name: coatnet_5_cls
members.extended:
Widest paper-cited variant — `dims=(256, 512, 1280, 2048)` — with
the standard CoAtNet head.  Approximately **688M parameters**,
85.8% ImageNet-1k top-1 at 224x224 (Table 5).

members.name: coatnet_6
members.extended:
Mixed-stage variant: S3 carries an MBConv sub-stage *and* a
transformer sub-stage at a wider dimension.  Spec from paper §A.2 /
Table 12:

- S0 conv stem: D=192, L=2 (handled by `stem_width`)
- S1 MBConv: L=2, D=192
- S2 MBConv: L=4, D=384
- S3 *mixed*: MBConv L=8 D=768  →  expand→1536  →  TFMRel L=42 D=1536
- S4 TFMRel: L=2, D=2048

Approximately **1.5B parameters** — only meaningful with very large
pretraining (the paper's headline 88.4-89.0% ImageNet numbers come
from JFT-3B pretraining + 512×512 finetune).
notes:
Footprint at default `image_size=224` exceeds what a 16 GB host
can comfortably instantiate (≈ 6 GB of params alone before
activations); use a larger GPU.  Building this variant is
reserved for inference on dedicated hardware.

members.name: coatnet_7
members.extended:
Widest mixed-stage variant.  Spec from paper §A.2 / Table 12:

- S0 conv stem: D=192, L=2
- S1 MBConv: L=2, D=256
- S2 MBConv: L=4, D=512
- S3 *mixed*: MBConv L=8 D=1024  →  expand→2048  →  TFMRel L=42 D=2048
- S4 TFMRel: L=2, D=3072

Approximately **2.4B parameters** — paper's strongest published
CoAtNet, only usable with JFT-3B pretraining.  Not buildable on a
16 GB host (param footprint alone is ≈ 9.7 GB before activations).

summary: CoAtNet family — backbone + image classification (Dai et al., 2021).
citation: Dai, Zihang, et al. "CoAtNet: Marrying Convolution and Attention for All Data Sizes." Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems, vol. 34, 2021.
theory:
CoAtNet hybridises depthwise convolution and relative self-attention
in a single backbone, motivated by the observation that
convolutions excel at *generalization* (strong local inductive
bias) while attention excels at *capacity* (data-dependent global
mixing).  The network is a four-stage pyramid preceded by a
two-layer convolutional stem; the early stages use *MBConv* blocks
(squeeze-and-excitation, expansion ratio 4) and the later stages
use *relative-attention* transformer blocks operating on
$\sqrt{HW}$-length token sequences.

The key building block of the transformer stages is *relative*
self-attention:



$$
\mathrm{Attn}(Q, K, V)_{ij} = \mathrm{softmax}\!\left(
    \frac{Q_i K_j^\top}{\sqrt{d}} + r_{i - j}
\right) V_j,
$$

where $r_{i-j}$ is a learned bias indexed by the *relative*
spatial offset between tokens.  This recovers the translation
equivariance that convolutions provide for free while still
permitting global, data-dependent mixing.  CoAtNet stacks two
MBConv stages (C-stages) followed by two transformer stages
(T-stages) so that strong local features are extracted first and
long-range dependencies are modelled at lower resolutions where
attention is cheap.  The CoAtNet-0 through CoAtNet-7 variants
simply scale stage depths and channel widths along this skeleton.
